description: did:key Ed25519 resolution. The input DID and its public verification key are taken verbatim from the W3C-CCG did:key method specification's worked Ed25519/X25519 example. The expected value is the verification key material the UniversalResolver yields (an Ed25519 OKP JWK for JWS verification) — a hardcoded spec literal, NOT recomputed by the impl on the expected side.

vector.note: The current did-method-key repo carries its vectors inline in index.html (there is no test-vectors/ directory on main); the Ed25519/X25519 worked example is the canonical published vector.

cases.description: Bare did:key DID (no fragment). Spec verificationMethod.publicKeyMultibase z6Mkha... is an Ed25519 key (multicodec 0xed01). The resolver strips the multicodec, base64url-encodes the 32-byte public key into JWK x, and synthesizes the kid as <did>#<identifier> (which equals the spec's verificationMethod.id).

verification.tool: pure-Python base58btc decoder + multicodec strip + base64url, implemented directly from the multiformats/did:key spec, NOT via the Rust impl
verification.note: JWK x literal independently derived from the spec's publicKeyMultibase; the harness asserts the impl's resolved JWK equals this literal.

cases.description: did:key DID with the spec's verificationMethod.id fragment. The resolver requires the fragment to equal the multibase identifier and returns the same key material.

keyAgreementX25519.reason: The spec example also derives an X25519 (multicodec 0xec01) keyAgreement key. The UniversalResolver intentionally resolves only Ed25519 signature keys for did:key (X25519 is a key-agreement, not JWS-verification, key), so it is not asserted here.
